title: Temperature & Humidity Sensor Calibration Drift Detection
app: Splunk Edge Hub (temperature + humidity sensors), Splunk OT Intelligence
spl:
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub sensor_type=temperature OR sensor_type=humidity
| stats latest(last_calibration_date) as last_cal, latest(sensor_drift_percent) as drift by sensor_type, host
| eval days_since_calibration=(now() - strptime(last_cal, "%Y-%m-%d")) / 86400
| eval calibration_status=case(
    drift > 5 OR days_since_calibration > 365, "OUT_OF_SPEC",
    drift > 2 OR days_since_calibration > 180, "MARGINAL",
    1=1, "GOOD")
| where calibration_status!="GOOD"
description: Detects when sensors exceed acceptable calibration drift to trigger preventive recalibration and ensure measurement accuracy.
value: Detects when sensors exceed acceptable calibration drift to trigger preventive recalibration and ensure measurement accuracy.
implementation: Edge Hub firmware tracks sensor calibration date and calculates drift estimate (comparison to stable reference or statistical baseline). Temperature sensor nominal accuracy ±0.2°C; alert if drift exceeds ±0.5°C (±2.5x drift). Humidity sensor nominal accuracy ±2%; alert if drift exceeds ±5% RH (±2.5x drift). Report calibration status to edge-hub-health every week. Recommend recalibration every 12 months or after >2% drift detected. Store calibration history in edge-hub-logs for audit trail. For critical environments (pharmaceutical, food), set more aggressive drift thresholds (±1% per year).
visualization: Sensor drift gauge, calibration status list, recalibration due timeline.
knownFalsePositives: Tag drift during recipe changes, batch transitions, or commissioning of new product runs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (temperature + humidity sensors), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-health` sourcetype=edge_hub sensor_calibration_status, `index=edge-hub-data` sensor_drift_metric.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Edge Hub firmware tracks sensor calibration date and calculates drift estimate (comparison to stable reference or statistical baseline). Temperature sensor nominal accuracy ±0.2°C; alert if drift exceeds ±0.5°C (±2.5x drift). Humidity sensor nominal accuracy ±2%; alert if drift exceeds ±5% RH (±2.5x drift). Report calibration status to edge-hub-health every week. Recommend recalibration every 12 months or after >2% drift detected. Store calibration history in edge-hub-logs for audit trail. For c…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub sensor_type=temperature OR sensor_type=humidity
| stats latest(last_calibration_date) as last_cal, latest(sensor_drift_percent) as drift by sensor_type, host
| eval days_since_calibration=(now() - strptime(last_cal, "%Y-%m-%d")) / 86400
| eval calibration_status=case(
    drift > 5 OR days_since_calibration > 365, "OUT_OF_SPEC",
    drift > 2 OR days_since_calibration > 180, "MARGINAL",
    1=1, "GOOD")
| where calibration_status!="GOOD"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Temperature & Humidity Sensor Calibration Drift Detection** — Detects when sensors exceed acceptable calibration drift to trigger preventive recalibration and ensure measurement accuracy.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-health` sourcetype=edge_hub sensor_calibration_status, `index=edge-hub-data` sensor_drift_metric. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (temperature + humidity sensors), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-health; **sourcetype**: edge_hub. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-health, sourcetype=edge_hub. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sensor_type, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since_calibration** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **calibration_status** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where calibration_status!="GOOD"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sensor drift gauge, calibration status list, recalibration due timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with uncomfortable or unsafe hot and cold spots before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
